before_code:
# Wrong: leave a deprecation shim at the old path in the same PR that moves the file
# (e.g. `module -- shake: keep-all` + `public import <new>` + `deprecated_module ...`).
after_code:
# Right: in the file-move PR, delete the old file outright (don't leave a stub).
# Run `lake exe mk_all` to drop the now-orphaned import from `Mathlib.lean`.
# Make a *separate, follow-up PR* immediately after the move that adds the
# deprecation shim back at the old path.
description: When moving a file in mathlib4: do NOT leave a deprecation shim at the old path in the same PR. Git's rename detection only kicks in when the old file *vanishes entirely* and an identically-content new file appears — that preserves history. A one-line stub at the old path defeats rename detection, so the new file shows up as freshly created and history is lost. The mathlib convention (per loefflerd, PR #38806) is: (1) in the move PR, just delete the old file outright (`git rm`), then `lake exe mk_all` to clean up `Mathlib.lean`; (2) in a *separate* immediate-follow-up PR, add the deprecation shim back at the old path with `deprecated_module "<message>" (since := "yyyy-mm-dd")` for downstream users. Tedious but preserves git blame/history.

before_code:
-- Renamed `ModularFormClass.qExpansion_smul` to `ModularForm.qExpansion_smul`,
-- old name is just gone
after_code:
-- After renaming, in the OLD namespace add:
namespace ModularFormClass

@[deprecated (since := "yyyy-mm-dd")]
protected alias qExpansion_smul := ModularForm.qExpansion_smul

-- ... etc for every renamed declaration

end ModularFormClass
description: When renaming declarations across namespaces (e.g. `Foo.bar` → `FooClass.bar`, or vice versa), don't just delete the old name — add `@[deprecated (since := "yyyy-mm-dd")] protected alias oldName := NewNamespace.newName` at the OLD namespace. `protected` keeps the old qualified-name only access. This applies to PRs that move/rename anything that downstream code might import.
